business.headline: Want to invest in SpaceX's IPO? Here's what retail investors need to knowAccess to the SpaceX IPO is not limited to the United States. The company has indicated that qualified investors in several countries may be able to buy shares, subject to local regulations and eligibility requirements.

Sensex, Nifty hit 2-month lows as Iran-Israel tensions push crude oil near $100Markets closed at nearly two-month lows after fresh Israeli strikes on Iran and Lebanon pushed crude prices sharply higher. The sell-off deepened across sectors as investors weighed inflation risks, global tech weakness and the path of interest rates.

Economist's model got last 3 FIFA World Cup winners right. Here's its 2026 pickAs football fans debate whether Argentina can defend its title, whether England's golden generation can finally deliver, or whether France can add another star to its jersey, a German economist has come up with a prediction that is turning heads across the football world.

From AI trip planning to travel insurance: How are Indians redefining travel in 2026?Gone are the days when a holiday meant simply booking tickets and packing bags. Today's Indian traveller is more digital, more informed and more prepared, reshaping the travel landscape in the process.

India, US could finalise trade deal by July, says Commerce Minister Piyush GoyalThe Union Minister's remarks come at a time when India and the US are working to resolve outstanding issues in a proposed bilateral trade pact that is expected to improve market access and give Indian exporters an advantage over competitors in certain sectors.

AI stock sell-off hits Taiwan, South Korea after rally past IndiaAsian markets sank as investors dumped AI-linked chip stocks in Taiwan, South Korea and Japan. The sell-off has raised fresh questions over whether AI rally expectations had run ahead of earnings reality.

CIBIL score under 730? Getting a home or car loan could become tougher from 2027For many Indians, a home loan or car loan is often the biggest financial commitment they make. But from 2027, getting such loans could become more difficult if your CIBIL score is below 730, as banks prepare to follow stricter RBI guidelines on lending risk.

Adani Ports bags 10-year marine services deal for Argentina's first LNG exportsThe contract was awarded to Adani Harbour International FZCO, a step-down subsidiary of APSEZ, through a consortium formed with Argentina-based Meridian Group. The consortium won the contract after a global competitive bidding process conducted by Southern Energy S.A. (SESA).

Gold falls by Rs 1,800, silver crashes Rs 6,400: Why are precious metals falling?Gold and silver extended losses on Monday as stronger US jobs data altered rate expectations. The slide deepened as higher Treasury yields, Middle East tensions and inflation worries weighed on bullion.

Sensex falls 600 points: 3 reasons why stock market is falling todayMarkets opened sharply lower on Monday as rising Middle East tensions, higher crude prices and global weakness hit sentiment. The slide sharpened concerns over inflation, foreign outflows and pressure on technology stocks.

Stock market falls amid fresh Iran-Israel flare up, crude oil climbs above $96The selloff was broad-based, with almost all major stocks trading in the red. Investors turned cautious after fresh conflict in the Middle East pushed oil prices higher and raised concerns about inflation and global economic growth.
Stock markets likely to fall today: 3 reasons whyGIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,138 at 8:06 am IST, indicating that the Nifty 50 could open about 1% lower than its previous close of 23,366.70.

Crude oil rises over 3%. Is $100 a barrel back in sight?Brent crude rose 3.45% to $96.30 per barrel, while US benchmark WTI crude climbed 3.41% to $93.63 per barrel during trading as of 8:10 am. The sharp rebound erased most of Friday's losses, when oil prices had fallen on hopes that diplomatic efforts could ease tensions in the region.

Your flat is delayed. Your EMI isn't. What now?You're paying the EMI. The builder missed the deadline. With over 5 lakh homes stuck across India, delayed possession has become a financial nightmare for thousands of buyers. Here's what experts say you can do when your dream home doesn't arrive on time.

Rosneft CEO predicts India will drive 50% of global oil demand growth by 2035Rosneft CEO cited International Energy Agency estimates projecting that India's oil consumption would reach nearly eight million barrels per day by 2035, representing growth of 44 per cent, while global demand overall would increase by about five per cent.

Indians have no will for a WILLA house. A few bank accounts. Some investments. Perhaps a family business. For many Indians, these assets represent decades of hard work. Yet surprisingly few leave behind clear instructions on who gets what, creating uncertainty for the very people they wanted to protect.

Oil India reports second gas discovery in Andaman offshore blockOil India has reported natural gas in the Vijayapuram-3 exploratory well in the Andaman offshore basin. The find adds to evidence of hydrocarbon potential in the block as appraisal work continues.

India-US trade deal's first tranche likely by mid-July, says Piyush GoyalSpeaking about the discussions, Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal said the meetings held between June 2 and June 4 were very encouraging.

India's economy grows 7.7% in FY26 despite global turmoil, war concernsProvisional government estimates show economic growth accelerated from the previous year, with robust expansion in the January-March quarter helping lift full-year GDP growth to 7.7%.

Sensex, Nifty end lower as RBI holds rates; rupee logs biggest gain since AprilMarkets ended lower after the RBI kept the repo rate at 5.25%, while the rupee posted its biggest one-day gain since April. Investors are now weighing inflation, crude prices, foreign outflows and West Asia tensions for clues on market direction.

entertainment.headline: Hina Khan says 'we all make mistakes' amid feud with Shilpa Shinde Actor Hina Khan reacted to the trolling around her ongoing public spat with Shilpa Shinde. Her appeal comes days after the two actors exchanged sharp remarks over Shinde's admission about filing false sexual harassment allegations.

Why Triptii Dimri's aunt cried after she bagged a film with Madhuri Dixit Triptii Dimri and Dharna Durga spoke about sharing screen space with Madhuri Dixit in Maa Behen. Dimri called it a personal milestone, while Dharna joked it should earn her more respect at home.

Don 3 row: CINTAA President Poonam Dhillon praises Ranveer for offering Rs 10 crore Poonam Dhillon has defended Ranveer Singh over his exit from Don 3 and praised his offer to compensate the makers. The CINTAA president also said that lead actors have every right to weigh in on scripts and casting decisions.

Don't blame Janhvi, blame the makers: Telugu actor Dimple Hayathi on Peddi row Telugu actor Dimple Hayathi responded to trolls questioning her support for Janhvi Kapoor during the Peddi row. She reflected on how female characters are written and portrayed on-screen.

The return of Triptii Dimri, the actor with a side of 1.27 lakh rotis in Maa Behen Triptii Dimri made a powerful performance-oriented comeback with Maa Behen. She delivered a darkly humorous and intense performance that revived her true acting prowess lost in Animal.

Kim Soo-hyun to resume work a year following Kim Sae-ron controversy: Report South Korean actor Kim Soo-hyun is reportedly set to return to work with a commercial shoot on July 14 for EIDER Philippines, marking his first official South Korean schedule since the controversy surrounding late actress Kim Sae-ron.

Karuppu on OTT: When and where to watch Suriya and Trisha's blockbuster film After breaking multiple box office records, Suriya and RJ Balaji's Karuppu is all set for its OTT debut. Following a successful theatrical run, the film is set to premiere on Prime Video in June.

'Darpok Madrasi man' R Madhavan on staying loyal to wife in 27 years of marriage R Madhavan has spoken about loyalty and trust in his marriage to Sarita. He said family values, openness and avoiding needless tests have shaped their relationship.

Javed Jaffrey's wife allegedly duped of Rs 16.24 crore, BMC official suspended The Assistant Municipal Commissioner of BMC, Mahesh Patil, has been suspended after Jaaved Jaaferi's wife Habiba Jaffrey accused him and others of cheating them in a Bandra redevelopment project.

Taylor Swift's courtside chair from NBA playoff game hits auction block A courtside seat linked to Taylor Swift's appearance at the Eastern Conference Finals is now up for auction. The unusual piece of memorabilia is already drawing attention from fans and collectors alike.

Toxic BTS clip shows the scale and pressure behind Geetu Mohandas' ambitious film A newly released behind-the-scenes video offers a rare look at Geetu Mohandas directing the ambitious film Toxic, revealing the scale, precision and intensity behind its large-scale action production.

Why Esha Deol's bed is covered in teddy bears after Dharmendra's death Esha Deol shared why her bed is filled with teddy bears after the death of her father Dharmendra, saying they help her feel closer to him.

Jaya Bachchan reacts to objectification of women in films amid Janhvi's Peddi row Jaya Bachchan has spoken out against the objectification of women in films amid the Peddi controversy. Her remarks come as criticism mounts over Janhvi Kapoor's portrayal in the Ram Charan-starrer.

Ram Charan, Buchi Babu watch Peddi amid Janhvi row, internet seeks accountability Ram Charan joined director Buchi Babu Sana for a surprise Peddi screening in Hyderabad. The visit has renewed scrutiny of the film as criticism over its portrayal of women continues.

Rakesh Roshan shuts down reports of Hrithik's Rs 500 crore demand for Krrish 4 Rakesh Roshan has rejected reports that Hrithik Roshan sought a Rs 500 crore budget for Krrish 4 and denied any disagreement with Aditya Chopra.

Toy Story 5 trailer: Woody and Buzz take backseat, Jessie leads a new adventure Toy Story 5 puts Jessie at the heart of a new adventure as the beloved cowgirl takes the lead. The film, releasing in India on June 19, 2026, explores playtime in an increasingly digital world.

Amid Peddi row, Sanjay Gupta says social media is destroying young actors' careers Sanjay Gupta reacted indirectly to the trolling over controversial scenes in Ram Charan's Peddi. He said social media is becoming a ruthless force for actors whose careers have only just begun.

Actor Belmont Cameli brings Garrett Graham moves to Kid LAROI concert, fans lose it Belmont Cameli surprised fans at The Kid LAROI's Los Angeles concert by breaking out some familiar moves. The viral moment has Off Campus fans revisiting one scene in particular.

Hai Jawani Toh... box office: Weekend boost takes Varun's film to Rs 23 crore Varun Dhawan and David Dhawan's Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai has held steady through its opening weekend. The film stayed ahead despite multiple releases, taking its worldwide gross to Rs 37.30 crore.

general news.headline: TMC's Bengal mutiny moves to Delhi, overshadows Mamata Banerjee's INDIA bloc pushAs Mamata Banerjee focused on projecting opposition unity at the INDIA bloc meeting in Delhi, her party appeared to be unraveling back in Bengal, with internal dissent weakening her authority and bargaining position both within the TMC and on the national stage.

Trump tells Israel and Iran to stop 'shooting' immediately as tensions remain highUS President Donald Trump urged Israel and Iran to immediately stop exchanging fire, insisting the latest hostilities would not derail ongoing US-Iran negotiations despite fresh Israeli strikes on Iranian military and energy targets and retaliatory Iranian missile attacks on Israel.

Alcohol sales allowed in Muslim-majority Lakshadweep. Explained why rules changedFor nearly five decades, Lakshadweep was one of India's few territories where alcohol sales were largely prohibited. Around 97% of the population is Muslim. The Centre is now changing that, bringing a regulated licencing system as part of a broader push to boost tourism.

Sukhendu Ray quits Trinamool; 20 party MPs meet Suvendu in new trouble for MamataAs Mamata Banerjee seeks to retain her bargaining power at the INDIA bloc meeting today, her leverage appears to be rapidly diminishing following the resignation of the party's Rajya Sabha chief whip, Sukhendu Sekhar Roy, and his subsequent meeting with several TMC MPs.

Babur's sultanate: Parasite on a prosperous civilisation?India was already one of the world's richest civilisations when Babur arrived in 1526. Five hundred years later, the question isn't about imperial splendour during the Timurid rule of Hindustan, but whether they extracted more wealth than they created.

Sonam Wangchuk from Ladakh. The lion, not the cockroachMany know Sonam Wangchuk, the climate activist and educationist, who backed the Cockroach Janta Party protest at Jantar Mantar. But not many know the other Sonam Wangchuk, who's also from Ladakh. Colonel Sonam Wangchuk, the 'Lion of Ladakh', fought in the icy heights during the 1999 Kargil War.

IAF to deploy Mi-17 helicopters to carry NEET re-test question papers: SourcesAhead of the June 21 NEET-UG re-test, the Indian Air Force will deploy Mi-17 helicopters and other assets to transport question paper packets from 18 locations, sources said. The move is part of enhanced security measures to ensure a leak-free examination.

India, US could finalise trade deal by July, says Commerce Minister Piyush GoyalThe Union Minister's remarks come at a time when India and the US are working to resolve outstanding issues in a proposed bilateral trade pact that is expected to improve market access and give Indian exporters an advantage over competitors in certain sectors.

CM Vijay plays chess with Praggnanandhaa, impresses Norway Chess championTamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay felicitated R Praggnanandhaa in Chennai, awarded him Rs 50 lakh and played an impromptu game of chess. The meeting followed the grandmaster's historic Norway Chess title win, with Praggnanandhaa saying he was surprised by the Chief Minister's skill.

These Indian cities could see auroras as solar storm hits Earth tonightThe sky over India is about to do something extraordinary. A massive solar storm is hitting Earth tonight, and auroras, popularly known as northern lights, are closer to home than you ever imagined. Here is everything you need to know before midnight.

Live: Tehran says it is ready for prolonged conflict in regionIsrael-Iran War Live Updates: The Israel-Iran conflict has entered a dangerous new phase, with both sides exchanging attacks across multiple fronts. Israel says it carried out fresh strikes on military targets in western and central Iran, while explosions were reported in Tehran, Tabriz and Isfahan. Iran has reportedly fired missiles at Israel for the first time since the April cease-fire, signalling a sharp escalation in hostilities. The tensions have also spilled beyond the two countries, with reports of Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon and Iranian military action in northern Iraq, raising fears of a wider regional conflict. Stay with IndiaToday.in for latest and live updates-

Indian Railways to roll out upgraded reservation system from AugustIndian Railways will begin shifting trains to its upgraded Passenger Reservation System from August. The new platform brings better booking capacity, AI-powered waitlist predictions and a smoother ticket booking experience for passengers.

This Italian pasta street is run by nonnas. They were also at the Ambani weddingA street in Italy dedicated to nonnas making and selling fresh pasta is a massive tourist attraction in Bari. Guess what, the nonnas were at the Ambani wedding as well!

TMC's Jahangir 'Pushpa' Khan arrested near Nepal border after weeks in hidingJahangir Khan, accused in multiple cases including alleged EVM tampering during the Bengal Assembly elections, was arrested after weeks on the run while trying to flee India.

Thank you, darling: Trump rips off mic, walks out of heated interviewDonald Trump walked out of NBC News' Meet the Press interview after clashing with Kristen Welker over 2020 presidential election fraud claims and California vote counting.

Video: Buildings collapse, streets shake as powerful quake strikes PhilippinesViral videos showed the southern Philippines earthquake shaking cities, collapsing buildings and driving seawater inland. The footage underscored the scale of damage, panic and tsunami fears as authorities issued alerts.
UP man marries mother-in-law, video of court ceremony goes viralA man and his mother-in-law allegedly got married in a court ceremony in Uttar Pradesh's Kanpur.

Economist's model got last 3 FIFA World Cup winners right. Here's its 2026 pickAs football fans debate whether Argentina can defend its title, whether England's golden generation can finally deliver, or whether France can add another star to its jersey, a German economist has come up with a prediction that is turning heads across the football world.

Mothership of Satan is in...: US envoy hides in Israel as Iran fires missilesIran fired another wave of missiles at Israel as US envoy Mike Huckabee sharply attacked Tehran. The exchange exposed contrasting messages from Washington while fears of a wider regional conflict grew.

Manav Suthar becomes 2nd Indian this century to pick 5 wickets on Test debutIndia's debutant left-arm spinner ripped through Afghanistan with 6/33 and helped enforce the follow-on. The spell, backed by a handy 28, put him among India's best Test debutants.

Andhra doctors perform brain surgery as patient watches Pawan Kalyan's OGA 45-year-old woman watched actor and Andhra Pradesh Deputy Chief Minister Pawan Kalyan's upcoming film OG while doctors performed a complex brain tumour surgery on her at a hospital in the Guntur district.

Gold falls by Rs 1,800, silver crashes Rs 6,400: Why are precious metals falling?Gold and silver extended losses on Monday as stronger US jobs data altered rate expectations. The slide deepened as higher Treasury yields, Middle East tensions and inflation worries weighed on bullion.

Vijay's TVK not invited to today's INDIA bloc meet, Congress explains whyAccording to sources, there is still no clarity on whether TVK will attend the meeting at Delhi's Constitution Club, and a decision on inviting actor-turned-politician Vijay's party is likely to be taken later today. However, the Congress, TVK's ally in Tamil Nadu, said that parties without MPs are not invited to attend the meeting.

El Nino effect: These cities will see dramatic changes in rain patternsA strong El Nino is building in the Pacific for June to August 2026, and the WMO expects it to reshape rainfall across the world's cities. From Delhi and Sydney to Johannesburg and the Ecuador coast, here is who faces drought and who faces downpours.

sports.headline: Innings and 300 runs: Suthar blows away Afghans inside 3 days on Test debut Shubman Gill's India registered their biggest victory in Test cricket on Monday, June 8. Debutant Manav Suthar blew away Afghanistan with 7 wickets in Mullanpur.

Ignored for India, Shami makes his presence felt with hattrick in Bengal T20 League Mohammed Shami took a Bengal T20 League hat-trick for Siliguri Strikers against Rarh Tigers. The spell revived scrutiny of India's selection calls as the veteran remains outside ODI and Test plans.

Angry Mohammed Siraj riles up Rahmat Shah, triggers Afghanistan collapse IND vs AFG: Mohammed Siraj triggered Afghanistan's collapse on Day 3 of the Test match. He did not do it with his bat or ball, but with his mere words.

India captain snaps back at reporter over retirement question at Women's T20 WC Harmanpreet Kaur brushed aside retirement talk with a joke at a pre-tournament captains' media session. The exchange kept the focus on India's T20 World Cup build-up and the wider lift success has given women's cricket at home.

CM Vijay plays chess with Praggnanandhaa, impresses Norway Chess champion Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay felicitated R Praggnanandhaa in Chennai, awarded him Rs 50 lakh and played an impromptu game of chess. The meeting followed the grandmaster's historic Norway Chess title win, with Praggnanandhaa saying he was surprised by the Chief Minister's skill.

Manav Suthar becomes 2nd Indian this century to pick 5 wickets on Test debut India's debutant left-arm spinner ripped through Afghanistan with 6/33 and helped enforce the follow-on. The spell, backed by a handy 28, put him among India's best Test debutants.

Let Sooryavanshi be, don't expect the world of him: Sourav Ganguly Former India captain Sourav Ganguly has asked the world to temper his expectations ahead of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi potential India debut. Ganguly has said that playing for India will be different from playing in the IPL.

Flavio Cobolli follows Andreeva's example, thanks himself after French Open final French Open 2026: Flavio Cobolli thanked himself and praised Alexander Zverev after a five-set French Open final defeat. The loss ended his title bid but confirmed a breakthrough fortnight that changed his standing in the game.

In Roland Garros, Zverev finds freedom in maiden Grand Slam win Germany's Alexander Zverev finally won his maiden Grand Slam. Playing against Italy's Flavio Cobolli, Zverev finally found the freedom that he had been looking for.

Why Praggnanandhaa's win in Norway Chess 2026 brings back belief of good times in Indian Chess? Norway Chess 2026: Praggnanandhaa created history when he won the Norway Chess 2026 event in grand style. Not only did he become the first Indian to win the prestigious tournament, he also defeated Magnus Carlsen in his sentinel twice and that deserves a special mention in the annals of chess.

Only Test, Day 3 Live: India win by innings and 100 runs in New ChandigarhIndia (IND) vs Afghanistan (AFG) Only Test Live Score and Updates, Day 3: It's all over in New Chandigarh. Afghanistan have lost by an innings and 300 runs. The Test match ends inside three days. Washington Sundar, Manav Suthar and Kuldeep Yadav run through the visitors' batting line-up.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and the summer of suspended disbeliefHandcrafted ByOur Editors In the summer of 2026, a boy from Bihar walked to the crease and gave Indian cricket back something it had quietly lost: the pure, uncomplicated joy of watching without needing to explain why.

Alexander Zverev leaves painful past behind, makes French Open his happy placeFrench Open 2026: Alexander Zverev has endured a difficult history at Roland Garros, including a serious injury in 2022 and a defeat in the 2024 final. But on Sunday, the German finally turned the page in Paris, capturing his first Grand Slam title and ending a long wait.

From losers to Grand Slam champions: Zverev pays emotional tribute to his teamAlexander Zverev finally ended his long pursuit of Grand Slam glory on Sunday, defeating Flavio Cobolli to win the French Open before thanking the team that stood beside him through injuries, heartbreak and repeated near-misses on tennis's biggest stages.

Neymar's last dance: Final shot at redemption or a farewell to Joga Bonito?FIFA World Cup 2026: With injuries recurring, Brazil's decision to bank on Neymar for what could be his final World Cup has reopened the old argument about promise, decline and necessity. Can Carlo Ancelotti's call turn a fading icon into the centre of Brazil's latest reckoning with hope and history?

Christian Eriksen collapses on pitch again, five years after cardiac arrestChristian Eriksen sparked fresh concern on Sunday after the Denmark midfielder collapsed on the pitch during an international friendly against Ukraine, forcing the match to be abandoned.

Watch: Zverev in tears, Cobolli graceful in defeat after marathon finalYears of near-misses, injury setbacks and Grand Slam heartbreak finally gave way to tears of joy as Alexander Zverev defeated Flavio Cobolli on Sunday to win the French Open and claim the first Major title of his career.

Fourth time lucky: Alexander Zverev breaks Grand Slam curse with French Open titleFrench Open 2026: Alexander Zverev had lost three Grand Slam finals earlier, but on Sunday, the German ended the streak with the first major of his career. On Sunday, the World No.3 took over four hours to beat Italy's Flavio Cobolli in a thrilling five-setter on Centre Court.

Pole vault king Mondo Duplantis suffers his first loss in nearly three yearsOlympic champion Armand 'Mondo' Duplantis suffered his first defeat in 35 months after Australia's Kurtis Marschall stunned the home crowd to win the pole vault competition at the Stockholm Diamond League.

Brazil call up Manchester United-linked Ederson after Wesley injuryBrazil have called up Atalanta midfielder Ederson to replace injured defender Wesley in their World Cup squad, with the late change offering Carlo Ancelotti an additional midfield option ahead of the tournament.

Kimi Antonelli makes it five wins in a row, becomes youngest to win Monaco GPKimi Antonelli became the youngest Monaco Grand Prix winner after overcoming a late red-flag restart to secure his fifth consecutive Formula One victory and extend his championship lead.

ENG vs NZ: Ben Stokes says Lord's pitch does little to help Test cricket's futureBen Stokes questioned the Lord's pitch after England's win over New Zealand, warning that extreme bowler-friendly surfaces could harm Test cricket's future and reduce the format's appeal.

Afghanistan coach rues costly DRS mistakes vs India: We were exceptionally rustyAfghanistan head coach Richard Pybus lamented his side's poor use of DRS, admitting they were "exceptionally rusty" and lacked the conviction needed to challenge key decisions.

Shreyas Iyer's sister pens heartfelt note after India T20I captaincy nodShresta Iyer shared an emotional Instagram post after Shreyas Iyer was named India's T20I captain. Her tribute underlined the setbacks, injuries and persistence behind his rise.

Manav Suthar's debut showed he can be a serious Test contender: Sunil GavaskarManav Suthar's impressive and accurate bowling on Test debut showed he has the potential to become a strong contender for a regular place in India's Test side, according to Sunil Gavaskar.

Manav Suthar's Test debut leaves father emotional: We were too nervous to watchManav Suthar's dream Test debut was a moment of immense pride for his family, but nerves and a touch of superstition meant they were not in the stands to witness the left-arm spinner's bowling heroics against Afghanistan in Mullanpur on Sunday.

Washington Sundar sees bright Test future for Manav SutharWashington Sundar praised debutant Manav Suthar after the left-arm spinner took three wickets against Afghanistan on day two in Mullanpur. Sundar said Suthar's skill, effort and attitude mark him out as a strong Test prospect for India.

Animesh Kujur breaches CWG 2026 qualifying standard, wins 200m silver in TaipeiNational record holder Animesh Kujur breached the qualifying standard for the 2026 Commonwealth Games after clinching silver in the men's 200m at the New Taipei City Athletics Open on Sunday.

Wish Bumrah committed himself for India: Ex-cricketer slams pick-and-choose approachSanjay Manjrekar criticized Jasprit Bumrah's on and off availability, questioning the premier pacer's commitment to the Indian national team after he was rested for India's upcoming T20I tour of the United Kingdom.

Pakistan Cricket Board mulls axing of Test coach Sarfaraz, captain Shan MasoodAfter Pakistan's 0-2 Test series loss to Bangladesh, the PCB is considering major changes to the team's set-up. Talks with Younis Khan and Mohammad Hafeez signal a wider overhaul, while head coach Sarfaraz Ahmed and captain Shan Masood are under pressure.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
